a.xlink:title: Dual closed-loop motor controller wrapping an L298N driver with two independent PID loops.
Central configuration: pin assignments, physical constants, and PID gains.
Sequential container that drives an ordered list of AutoStep objects.
Top-level robot class that owns all subsystems and the autonomous scheduler.
AutoStep that runs a heap-owned sequence of child steps in order.
Differential-drive subsystem: closed-loop wheel control, line following, wall-distance holding,...
Miner subsystem: servo-driven block-pressing mechanism and ramp deployment.
Rack-and-pinion block shooter subsystem.
Implementation of the differential-drive odometry integrator.
High-level AutoStep that runs the full strategy loop inline.
Arduino entry point — instantiates the Robot and drives the main loop.
High-level autonomous planner used with ReplanStep.
Implementation of the autonomous Planner state machine.
AutoStep that drives straight while ramping speed up to a target velocity.
Instantaneous AutoStep that advances the Strategy craft cycle by one.
Instantaneous AutoStep that deploys the miner's block-collection ramp.
AutoStep that samples the color sensor and updates Strategy with the detected block type.
AutoStep that drives the robot along a circular arc.
AutoStep that drives straight for a specified distance at a fixed velocity.
AutoStep that follows a line until the distance sensor reads a target wall distance.
AutoStep that drives along an arc for a specified heading change (degrees).
AutoStep that drives along a constant-radius arc for a specified arc length.
AutoStep that drives straight toward a wall until the distance sensor reaches a setpoint.
AutoStep that follows a line for a specified distance.
AutoStep that routes a newly acquired block to the correct hopper slot.
AutoStep that commands the Miner to complete a fixed number of press cycles.
AutoStep that mines and simultaneously autofires the shooter for a time window.
AutoStep stub that places a block on the crafting table.
AutoStep stub that executes the block-rejection sequence.
AutoStep that blocks for a fixed number of milliseconds.
AutoStep that fires the shooter for a fixed duration, then stops it.
